seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Instructor simplifies structured output extraction from LLMs by patching clients to return validated Pydantic models, perfect for RAG pipelines needing precise document synthesis and citation extraction. Guardrails are essential in RAG to prevent hallucinations, ensure source faithfulness, and maintain citation accuracy in high-risk applications. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before they impact users.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
A typical Instructor + RAG pipeline starts with vector search over document embeddings to retrieve relevant chunks. These chunks are assembled into context and passed to an Instructor-patched LLM client that extracts structured responses with citations. The Pydantic models enforce schemas for answer content, sources, and confidence scores.

Instructor wraps OpenAI's chat.completions.create() to validate outputs against models like AnswerWithSources, retrying on failures. Vex verification layers add runtime checks for hallucination, relevance, and faithfulness post-extraction.

This architecture ensures type-safe, verifiable responses while maintaining RAG's core retrieval-generation flow.

risks.name: Source Faithfulness Failure
risks.description: LLM generates claims not grounded in retrieved context
risks.mitigation: Vex faithfulness checks trace every claim to source chunks

risks.name: Hallucinated Citations
risks.description: Model fabricates source IDs or references non-existent chunks
risks.mitigation: Validate citation indices against actual retrieved documents

risks.name: Context Relevance Drift
risks.description: Irrelevant chunks passed to LLM degrade answer quality
risks.mitigation: Pre-filter chunks with semantic similarity thresholds

risks.name: Structured Extraction Collapse
risks.description: Instructor retries exhausted, partial Pydantic models returned
risks.mitigation: Vex fallback assertions ensure minimum required fields

implementation.title: Pydantic Model for RAG Response
implementation.description: Define structured output schema with citations and confidence
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Optional

class Citation(BaseModel):
    chunk_id: int
    start_char: int
    end_char: int
    content: str = Field(..., max_length=500)

class RAGResponse(BaseModel):
    answer: str = Field(..., max_length=1000)
    citations: List[Citation] = Field(..., min_items=1, max_items=5)
    confidence: float = Field(..., ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    sources_used: int = Field(..., ge=1)

implementation.title: Instructor Client Setup
implementation.description: Patch OpenAI client and create RAG response function

implementation.title: Basic Vector Search Integration
implementation.description: Retrieve and format context chunks for Instructor
implementation.code:
import pinecone

index = pinecone.Index('rag-docs')

async def retrieve_context(query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> str:
    query_embedding = get_embedding(query)  # Your embedding function
    results = await index.query(
        vector=query_embedding,
        top_k=top_k,
        include_metadata=True
    )
    chunks = []
    for match in results['matches']:
        chunks.append(f"[Source {match['id']}]: {match['metadata']['text']}")
    return "\n".join(chunks)
